channel.title: Linux on mbtamuli's scratchpad
channel.description: Recent content in Linux on mbtamuli's scratchpad

item.title: Route VPN Traffic
item.description:
My personal laptop, my beloved Dell XPS 15, broke down. The SSD was not being detected. Fixing it would take time and I thought I’d get it fixed when I less workload.

Now as part of my work, I need to connect to a VPN. The VPN solution is Palo Alto’s GlobalProtect VPN 😅 (I’ll rant about GlobalProtect VPN some other time!). I can only connect to the VPN using my Dell XPS as it’s MAC address was registered. I got another laptop lying around(ThinkPad E470), but the process to get this new laptop’s MAC address registered would take around 2 weeks!

item.title: Backup files to git repo before shutdown
item.description:
I have a few files that I want to push to a remote repository just before shutdown. It could be many use cases, but mine is, I want my dotfiles synced to mbtamuli/dotfiles.

Well, the approach is simple

  • setup a small script to do git push
  • have a systemd service
  • What? You thought there’d be a ton of steps? Sorry to dissapoint. 😁
  • profit! 😎

Create another file and do whatever you need /usr/local/bin/backup_work.sh

item.title: Configuring Postfix
item.description:
I came across this really neat Docker image docker-postfix. It got me up and running with Postfix in a very short amount of time.

I’m just mentioning the extra steps I had to take to get the TLS certificates and the DKIM keys. As this is a post of configuring Postfix running as a Docker container, I’ll also run all the setup steps inside containers.

First let’s create a directory for all our files and pull all the images

item.title: Postfix in a container
item.description:
Postfix is a service which forks itself and runs in the background. Postfix version 3.3.0 added container support. There was an issue where Postfix could not run as PID=1, and it was swiftly fixed in version 3.3.1

As I could not find any official Docker image for the latest versin of Postfix, I decided to build one myself. At the time I built the image, there was no Debian package available so I had to compile the package from source.
